note: Reviewed the 13 near-dup clusters (E5 >=0.93). 'merge' = near-identical teaching repeated across chapters; keep the survivor, fold the other's verses in. 'keep' = related but a genuinely distinct facet. 'review' = subtle, your call.

recommendations.why: Equal-vision recurs 5x. Keep three real facets: seeing the Self in all (142), worshipping the one presence (143), the indestructible reality (336). 87 duplicates 142; 248 duplicates 336.
Divine-in-all recurs 3x. 163 (in everything you see) and 194 (behind every form) are distinct facets; 81 (in every act) is close to 163 — merge or keep, your call.
26 = rise above opposites AND possessiveness (2:45); 284 = rise above pride and delusion (15:5). Different vices — keep both.
'Offer all actions to the divine' — 3:30 and 18:57 are the same teaching restated. Merge; keep 61, add 18:57.
'Do your own duty poorly over another's well' — verbatim Gita repetition (3:35 & 18:47). Merge; keep 64, add 18:47.
96 = knowledge and action are one path (5:5); 152 = pair knowledge with lived realization, not theory (7:2). Distinct points — keep both.
'Knowledge burns away ignorance' (sun 5:16 / lamp 10:11) — same metaphor. Merge into the lamp phrasing (208), add 5:16.
'Renounce selfish desire, not the action' — 6:2 and 18:2 are the same. Merge; keep 324 (18:2, the canonical statement), add 6:2.
165 = whatever you worship with faith grows (7:21); 308 = you become the faith you hold (17:3). Related but different angle — keep both.
'Surrender your whole being' — 12:7 and 18:62 restate one idea. Merge; keep 364, add 12:7.
'Fix your mind on the divine' — 12:8 and 18:65 are the same instruction. Merge; keep 366, add 12:8.
239 = serve your teacher as a mark of knowledge (13:8); 315 = honor teachers / bodily austerity (17:14). Different contexts — keep both.
'Never do good merely for show' — 16:17 and 17:12 coincide. Merge; keep 314, add 16:17.
